text: <par>This isn't a loan. ho- reflects PTB *s(y)a-, and rə- < PT *ra, cf. Pasi sira 'boar; wild pig'.</par>
<par>Possible Indic loan, cf. Assamese gahori, suwor.</par>

<par>This is an irregular form, but probably not a loan (what would the source be?). It looks more like a carryover from taboo, hunting, or ritual language, possibly reflecting substrate vocabulary.</par>
<par>This entry was extracted from the index. Full definition: “<latinform>alə</latinform> <emph>n.</emph> good thing; merit."</par>
<par>This is a bit of a strange form, and not well-attested in Tani. It would be interesting if it were a loan, but from what lg?</par>
<par>Possible loan from Indic, cf. Assamese gahori, suwor. cf. 161076</par>
